column1: ## RUN 1 — default (SCHED_OTHER, no pinning, no RT) — BASELINE
## RUN 2 — SCHED_FIFO 50 + pinned to CPU 6 (matches absgrid runtime conditions)
# Headline: at every requested interval (100µs..5ms), SCHED_FIFO+pinned overshoots
# by only 2-3µs. p99 = p50 + 1µs. CONFIG_HZ=1000 rounding hypothesis FALSIFIED.
# Kernel sleep is fine; the absgrid 1.435ms floor is engine-side.
# Q: was idle=poll the confound that made absgrid fail on 5/10?
# A: NO. absgrid alone (stock kernel, no idle=poll) reproduces the bug.
## ATL:27019 with -absgrid (no idle=poll, stock kernel) — 6 KTP_PROFILE samples
## Same-host control instances at same time (ATL:27015,27018; no -absgrid)
# Conclusion: absgrid bug is engine-side, reproducible on stock kernel.
# 5/10's idle=poll was not the cause; both 5/10 (with idle=poll) and tonight
# (without) measured ~690 fps under absgrid. The ~290 fps regression vs
# default pingboost-2 (~980 fps) is in the launcher loop or the engine's
# Host_Frame work, not in clock_nanosleep itself.
#   RunFrame work     ~0.016ms  (per KTP_PROFILE 'full' on empty server)
# Required next step: instrumented build of KTPReHLDS that logs per-iteration
# sleep + work + total times to find where the 410µs sink lives.
